sectionOrderRules: Hero must include business type and a primary CTA to contact or request transport inquiry.
Service summary must describe actual air transport offerings or assignment types, not generic claims.
Trust proof must use real company facts such as organisation number visibility, references or clear operating information.
Operations summary may appear before trust proof when geographic coverage or traffic type is central to understanding the offer.
Sections should avoid repeating the same safety or reliability claim in multiple blocks.
Services intro must connect the company's air transport role to the listed services.
Service list items must be concrete, for example charter, freight, specialist transport or related operational services when actually offered.
Pricing may only describe pricing principles or request-for-quote flow unless exact prices are genuinely available.
Coverage-or-routes must describe real destinations, service areas, traffic patterns or operational scope without implying unavailable routes.
Process must explain how an inquiry, planning step or transport assignment typically proceeds.
Map should support location understanding and not falsely suggest live route tracking or airport partnerships.
About story must be specific to the company and its operations, not generic corporate language.
Team section may show key people, flight operations staff or customer-facing roles when available.
Trust proof must stay factual and must not invent certifications, fleet size or flight volume.
Phone, email and address must be present when available and must not be placeholders.
Contact CTA should support inquiry or booking request expectations clearly without promising instant confirmation unless that process exists.
Opening hours should reflect real contact availability, not assumed 24/7 service.
